commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Expose WhatsApp responsiveness doctor lint findings
target.body:
## Summary
- add default-disabled `core/doctor/whatsapp-responsiveness` structured lint findings
- reuse the existing WhatsApp responsiveness signal: WhatsApp enabled + degraded Gateway event loop + local TUI clients
- keep real cleanup in the existing `doctor --fix` path; lint only reports the actionable pressure state

## Public contract
- No config schema, plugin manifest, SDK export, persisted data-model, or public plugin contract changes.
- The new check is opt-in for broad lint via `defaultEnabled: false`; default `doctor --lint` remains unchanged.

## Validation
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.ts src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.ts src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-whatsapp-responsiveness.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm tsgo:core`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- Source CLI with isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, WhatsApp enabled:
  - `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json` exited 1 because unrelated default lint findings were present, ran 25 checks, and emitted 0 `core/doctor/whatsapp-responsiveness` findings.
  - `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/whatsapp-responsiveness` exited 0, ran 1 check, and emitted 0 WhatsApp responsiveness findings when the Gateway did not provide a degraded event-loop status.
- Focused source tests prove the positive selected-lint signal: WhatsApp enabled + `eventLoop.degraded === true` + local TUI process emits a warning finding with `requirement: "local-tui-event-loop-pressure"` and a `doctor --fix` hint.
